scenario_description: A user tracks a specific spiderweb fracture on a stained glass 'Moon Goddess' eye across multiple views, including a zoomed-out window shot and floor tiles, before asking for the fracture's orientation relative to the eyebrow from a new angle.

turns.text: Look at this crack in the center of the eye. It looks like a spiderweb radiating outwards.
turns.image_description: A tight close-up of a stained glass eye belonging to a figure labeled 'Moon Goddess'. The iris is deep blue with silver flecks. A distinct, jagged white fracture originates exactly at the pupil (center) and extends in a starburst pattern. One prominent leg of this fracture points sharply towards the upper-left corner of the frame, angling directly under the arch of the dark grey eyebrow.

turns.text: That is a very distinct spiderweb pattern. The main branch heading toward the top left seems to be the most prominent part of that fracture.
Here is the whole window context. You can see the floor tiles below it too.
turns.image_description: A wide shot of a tall, arched stained glass window set into a stone wall. The 'Moon Goddess' face occupies the top third of the window. Below the face are decorative floral patterns, and further down, near the bottom of the window, are geometric tile designs. On the floor in front of the window, there are large hexagonal terracotta tiles. The fracture seen previously is barely visible as a tiny white speck in the eye due to the distance.

turns.text: Okay, I see the full scale of the window now. The floor tiles look quite old, and the Goddess is definitely high up.
I moved closer again but shifted my position slightly. Can you describe the fracture's orientation relative to the eyebrow now?
turns.image_description: A medium close-up of the 'Moon Goddess' face, taken from a slightly lower angle than Turn 1. The perspective has shifted so the right side of the face is more prominent. The same spiderweb fracture is visible in the eye. Due to the change in angle, the fracture leg that was pointing to the upper-left in the first image now appears to point almost directly upward, slightly tilting towards the viewer's right, running parallel to the vertical edge of the eyebrow rather than angling under it.

turns.text: The angle makes the fracture look different here; it seems to run much more vertically compared to the first shot.

Based on the sequence of images, which description best matches the primary leg of the spiderweb fracture relative to the eyebrow? A) It always angles sharply under the left side of the eyebrow. B) It points away from the eyebrow entirely, towards the cheek. C) Its apparent orientation shifts from angling under the eyebrow to running nearly parallel/upward due to the camera angle change. D) It forms a perfect circle around the pupil, unrelated to the eyebrow.

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: Turn 1 shows the fracture leg angling sharply under the left side of the eyebrow. Turn 5 shows the same entity from a different angle where that same leg appears to run nearly parallel or upward relative to the eyebrow. Option C correctly synthesizes these two visual states to explain the shift. Option A only describes Turn 1. Option B describes a feature not present in either turn. Option D describes a shape not present.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The model must track the specific 'spiderweb fracture' entity across three turns, ignore the distractor of the floor tiles in Turn 3, and recognize that the change in spatial relationship described in the final question is caused by the change in camera angle between Turn 1 and Turn 5, rather than a physical change in the object.

_meta.scenario: The user starts by pointing out a specific spiderweb fracture radiating from the center of the 'Moon Goddess' eye. They then zoom out to show the whole window, pan down to the floor tiles, and zoom back into the 'Moon Goddess' face but from a slightly different angle, asking for a description of the fracture's orientation relative to the eyebrow.
